req.title: Manager creates employee
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) a happy-path where a manager with valid credentials submits all mandatory fields and receives 200/201 plus an audit record; (2) validation-error paths where mandatory fields are missing or malformed (400); (3) auth/authz error paths (401, 403); (4) boundary conditions on string-length fields. I selected five tests covering EP (valid partition), NEG (missing field, unauthenticated, forbidden role), BVA (max-length name field), and DT (decision table over role x auth combinations). Each test uses a dedicated manager session fixture and cleans up created employees via a teardown helper to stay idempotent.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_employee_success
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
No existing employee with the test email
actions: POST /employees with all mandatory fields populated with valid data
Query audit log for the returned entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record exists for the created employee id
trace: R-WEB-001: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_creates_employee_success(manager_session, cleanup_employee, audit_log_has_record):
    payload = {
        "first_name": "Alice",
        "last_name": "Smith",
        "email": "alice.smith.test@example.com",
        "department": "Engineering",
        "start_date": "2024-01-15"
    }
    resp = manager_session.post("/employees", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    cleanup_employee(entity_id)
    assert audit_log_has_record(entity_id, action="CREATE"), (
        f"No audit record found for employee {entity_id}"
    )

tests.name: test_create_employee_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
actions: POST /employees with the mandatory 'email' field omitted
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-001: Mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_employee_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(manager_session):
    payload = {
        "first_name": "Bob",
        "last_name": "Jones",
        # 'email' intentionally omitted
        "department": "HR",
        "start_date": "2024-03-01"
    }
    resp = manager_session.post("/employees", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    errors = resp.json()
    error_text = str(errors).lower()
    assert "email" in error_text, (
        "Validation error should reference the missing 'email' field"
    )

tests.name: test_create_employee_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: No session / bearer token is provided
actions: POST /employees with a valid payload but no auth header

tests.name: test_create_employee_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'viewer' (not manager)
actions: POST /employees with a valid payload using a viewer-role session

tests.name: test_create_employee_max_length_name_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
System allows last_name up to 100 characters
actions: POST /employees with last_name set to exactly 100 characters
POST /employees with last_name set to 101 characters
expected: 100-character last_name: status 200 or 201 with entity id
trace: R-WEB-001: Mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-001: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_employee_max_length_name_boundary(manager_session, cleanup_employee):
    base = {
        "first_name": "Dana",
        "email": "dana.boundary.test@example.com",
        "department": "Ops",
        "start_date": "2024-09-01"
    }
    exact_max = "X" * 100
    resp_ok = manager_session.post("/employees", json={**base, "last_name": exact_max})
    assert resp_ok.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"100-char last_name should succeed, got {resp_ok.status_code}"
    )
    entity_id = resp_ok.json().get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must include entity id"
    cleanup_employee(entity_id)
    over_max = "X" * 101
    resp_err = manager_session.post("/employees", json={**base, "last_name": over_max})
    assert resp_err.status_code == 400, (
        f"101-char last_name should fail with 400, got {resp_err.status_code}"
    )
